title: Tech layoffs surge, big tech freezes hiring, optimizing for profits, election preview & more
description:
(0:00) Jason's new brand deal

(1:18) Bestie updates

(9:12) "Ligma/Johnson" blunder outside of Twitter HQ

(15:38) Surge of tech layoffs at Twitter, Stripe, Lyft, Opendoor, Chime and others; preparing for a longer downturn than originally anticipated

(35:28) Macro trends, big tech freezes hiring, how founders can think about the last 3 years and the next 3 years

(54:04) Midterm election preview, understanding the shift toward populism

(1:17:32) Science corner: Understanding Meta's AlphaFold competitor

analysis.summary: The episode covers the surge of tech layoffs, big tech freezing hiring, and optimizing for profits. The hosts also discuss the upcoming midterm election and the shift toward populism. Additionally, they touch on macro trends and how founders can think about the last 3 years and the next 3 years.

takeaways.text: The shift toward populism is growing
Meta's AlphaFold competitor is a significant development
The labor force participation rate is declining
Economists may be underestimating the labor force participation rate
AI protein research could drive progress in medicine and clean energy

sentiment.notes: The hosts discuss the surge of tech layoffs and the shift toward populism, indicating a cautious tone. They also touch on the importance of optimizing for profits, which suggests a sense of uncertainty in the market.

notable_moments.description: Big tech hiring freeze is discussed
